signals.headline: Two-week ceasefire agreed between US and Iran | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Two-week ceasefire agreed between US and Iran | YES | HIGH confidence**
The United States and Iran agreed to a **two-week ceasefire on April 8, 2026**, mediated by Pakistan, with the agreement announced by President Trump.[1] However, the ceasefire has already been violated by both sides since its declaration.[1]

signals.raw:
Strait of Hormuz reopening committed | YES | HIGH confidence**
Iran agreed to **immediately open the Strait of Hormuz** as part of ceasefire conditions, following Trump's threat to "erase a whole civilization" if the strategic shipping chokepoint remained closed.[2]

signals.headline: Peace negotiations in Islamabad stalled | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Peace negotiations in Islamabad stalled | YES | HIGH confidence**
US negotiating team led by Vice President JD Vance arrived in Islamabad on April 11 for talks with Iranian officials, but the negotiations **ended without progress by April 12**.[3] Vance indicated Iran firmly rejected the US proposal to permanently end its nuclear program.[3]
